title: alangium salviifolium subsp. hexapetalum
intro: Alangium salviifolium subsp. hexapetalum, also known as the six-petaled alangium, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Alangiaceae. This plant is widely used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The medicinal properties of Alangium salviifolium subsp. hexapetalum are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various respiratory and digestive issues, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from minor skin irritations to more serious infections.
